proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment-arm columns (treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine, treatment_7plus3) each differ from non-recipients in objective_response when tested marginally without regard to biomarker status.

analyses.code: for t in treatments: chi2 of objective_response ~ t
analyses.result_summary: Marginal ORR differences are tiny: midostaurin 16.86% vs 16.91% (diff -0.05pp), gilteritinib 16.30% vs 17.01% (-0.71pp), ivosidenib 17.09% vs 16.89% (+0.20pp), enasidenib 16.11% vs 16.97% (-0.86pp), venetoclax_azacitidine 17.35% vs 16.61% (+0.74pp, p=0.04), 7+3 17.02% vs 16.81% (+0.21pp). Only ven+aza shows a nominal main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with idh1_mutation = 1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with idh1_mutation = 0 (positive prognostic effect).
Patients with tp53_mutation = 1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with tp53_mutation = 0 (negative prognostic effect, consistent with AML literature).
Patients with complex_karyotype = 1 have a lower objective_response rate than complex_karyotype = 0.
Patients with secondary_aml = 1 have a lower objective_response rate than secondary_aml = 0.
Patients with npm1_mutation = 1 have a higher objective_response rate than npm1_mutation = 0.

analyses.result_summary: IDH1+ patients (n=3624) have ORR 22.13% vs 16.50% in IDH1- (n=46376); difference +5.63 percentage points, chi-square p=3.5e-18. Strong positive prognostic main effect for IDH1.
TP53+ ORR 16.57% vs TP53- 16.94%, diff -0.37pp, p=0.52. No detectable prognostic effect of TP53 mutation in this cohort, contrary to typical AML literature.
Complex karyotype+ ORR 16.81% vs CK- 16.92%, diff -0.12pp, p=0.82. No marginal effect.
Secondary AML+ ORR 16.87% vs primary 16.92%, diff -0.05pp, p=0.92. No effect.
NPM1+ ORR 17.32% vs NPM1- 16.73%, diff +0.59pp, p=0.11. Trend toward positive prognostic but not significant marginally.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response (negative effect of poor performance status).
Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response (positive nutritional/inflammatory marker).
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: ORR 21.29% (ECOG 0), 15.52% (ECOG 1), 11.19% (ECOG 2). Logistic logOR per unit ECOG = -0.39, p<1e-50. Strong negative gradient.
Per 1 SD increase in marrow blast %, logOR = -0.109, p=1.2e-19. Higher tumor burden predicts lower response.
Per 1 SD WBC, logOR = -0.056, p=1.9e-05. Higher leukocyte count predicts lower response.
Per 1 SD albumin, logOR = +0.058, p=1.4e-06. Higher albumin predicts higher response (favorable nutritional status).
Per 1 SD CRP, logOR = -0.064, p=2.8e-06. Higher inflammation predicts lower response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among FLT3-ITD positive patients, treatment_midostaurin recipients have higher objective_response than non-recipients (FLT3-targeted therapy benefit in mutation-positive subgroup).
Among FLT3-ITD positive patients, treatment_gilteritinib recipients have higher objective_response than non-recipients.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_midostaurin and flt3_itd on objective_response (treatment effect larger in mutation-positive patients).

analyses.result_summary: In FLT3-ITD+ subgroup (n=10000): midostaurin ORR 16.13% (n=1468) vs 17.41% off (n=8532), diff -1.28pp, p=0.27. No benefit detected.
In FLT3-ITD+: gilteritinib ORR 16.90% (n=1491) vs 17.21% off (n=8509), diff -0.31pp, p=0.78. No benefit.
Interaction logOR = -0.105, p=0.22. No evidence of positive interaction; trend is actually slightly negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among IDH1-mutated patients, treatment_ivosidenib recipients have higher objective_response than non-recipients (target-matched therapy).
There is a positive interaction between treatment_ivosidenib and idh1_mutation on objective_response.
Among IDH2-mutated patients, treatment_enasidenib recipients have higher objective_response than non-recipients.

analyses.result_summary: Among IDH1+ (n=3624): ivosidenib ORR 17.45% (n=298) vs 22.55% off (n=3326), diff -5.10pp, p=0.050. Direction is OPPOSITE expected (no benefit; suggestive of paradoxical lower response on ivosidenib).
Interaction logOR = -0.364, p=0.027. Significant NEGATIVE interaction: ivosidenib's effect is more negative in IDH1+ than IDH1-, opposite of clinically expected pattern.
Among IDH2+ (n=4679): enasidenib ORR 14.41% (n=354) vs 16.74% off (n=4325), diff -2.33pp, p=0.29. No benefit detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among unfit_for_intensive=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine recipients have higher objective_response than non-recipients.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and unfit_for_intensive on objective_response.
Among fit (unfit_for_intensive=0) patients, treatment_7plus3 recipients have higher objective_response than non-recipients.

analyses.result_summary: Unfit subgroup (n=20056): ven+aza ORR 17.93% (n=8013) vs 16.27% off (n=12043), diff +1.66pp, p=0.0029. Significant benefit.
Interaction logOR = +0.104, p=0.036. Treatment effect is larger in unfit patients than fit patients, as clinically expected.
Fit subgroup (n=29944): 7+3 ORR 17.07% (n=13474) vs 16.67% off (n=16470), diff +0.40pp, p=0.37. No detectable benefit of 7+3 in fit patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and npm1_mutation on objective_response (greater treatment benefit in NPM1-mutated patients).
Among NPM1-mutated patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine recipients have higher objective_response than non-recipients.
Among NPM1-wildtype patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no benefit relative to non-recipients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction logOR = +0.261, p=6.65e-07. Highly significant positive interaction: venetoclax+azacitidine effect is much larger in NPM1+ patients.
NPM1+ subgroup (n=15014): ven+aza ORR 19.34% (n=6025) vs 15.96% off (n=8989), diff +3.38pp, p=9.8e-08. Strong, highly significant benefit.
NPM1- subgroup (n=34986): ven+aza ORR 16.49% (n=13995) vs 16.88% off (n=20991), diff -0.39pp, p=0.34. No benefit in NPM1-wildtype.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a negative interaction between treatment_7plus3 and complex_karyotype on objective_response (7+3 underperforms in adverse-risk cytogenetics).
Among complex_karyotype=1 patients, treatment_7plus3 recipients have lower objective_response than non-recipients.
Among complex_karyotype=0 patients, treatment_7plus3 shows a non-negative effect.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction logOR = -0.213, p=1.6e-03. Significant negative interaction: 7+3 effect is worse in complex-karyotype patients.
Complex karyotype subgroup (n=7462): 7+3 ORR 15.55% (n=3415) vs 17.87% off (n=4047), diff -2.32pp, p=0.0084. Significant lower response on 7+3 in CK+ patients.
CK- subgroup (n=42538): 7+3 ORR 17.28% (n=19165) vs 16.63% off (n=23373), diff +0.66pp, p=0.075. Trend positive but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: race_ethnicity is associated with objective_response (response rates differ across racial/ethnic groups).
White patients have higher objective_response than non-White patients (Black + Asian + Hispanic + other combined).
After adjustment for ECOG, age, biomarkers and labs, the racial differences in objective_response disappear.

analyses.result_summary: ORR by race: white 17.28%, hispanic 16.70%, asian 16.33%, black 15.74%, other 14.72%. Chi-square p=0.010. Significant heterogeneity.
White ORR 17.28% (n=32498) vs non-white 16.30% (n=17502), diff +0.98pp, p=0.005. White patients have a higher response rate marginally.

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ race + age + ecog_ps + idh1 + npm1 + labs)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted race effects (logOR vs reference asian): black -0.04 p=0.55, hispanic +0.04 p=0.52, other -0.11 p=0.27, white +0.08 p=0.13. None significant after adjustment, suggesting marginal racial differences are explained by composition rather than independent effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: insurance_type (medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured) is associated with objective_response.
rural_residence=1 patients have a different objective_response than urban patients.
Higher education_years is associated with higher objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: ORR by insurance: medicare 17.18%, medicaid 16.87%, private 16.66%, uninsured 16.62%. Chi-square p=0.54. No significant difference.
Rural ORR 16.91% vs urban 16.90%, diff +0.01pp, p=1.00. No effect.
Per SD education, logOR = +0.001, p=0.92. No association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Common medical comorbidities (diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, coronary_artery_disease, prior_malignancy) each affect objective_response.
Patient-reported symptom grades (fatigue, pain, dyspnea, cough, appetite_loss) are negatively associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Effects (diff in ORR, p): diabetes +0.40pp p=0.33; hypertension -0.23pp p=0.51; copd -0.56pp p=0.14; CKD +0.78pp p=0.13; HF -0.21pp p=0.72; CAD -0.33pp p=0.46; prior_malignancy +0.53pp p=0.31. None significant.

analyses.code: Logit per SD for each symptom grade
analyses.result_summary: Per SD logOR: fatigue +0.015 p=0.21; pain +0.011 p=0.34; dyspnea -0.022 p=0.07 (trend); cough -0.011 p=0.35; appetite_loss -0.025 p=0.038 (significant negative). Only appetite loss reaches nominal significance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 24 SNP genotype indicators (snp_*) is associated with objective_response at nominal p<0.05.
snp_rs1050828 (G6PD-related) is associated with higher objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Lowest p-value: snp_rs1050828 (diff +1.72pp, p=0.0056). Other near-nominal: rs2032582 -0.67pp p=0.051; rs1799853 -1.08pp p=0.057; rs3813867 +1.44pp p=0.064. Only rs1050828 survives nominal threshold; all fail Bonferroni correction (0.05/24=0.0021).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, biomarkers, labs and treatments in a multivariable logistic regression, ecog_ps remains the strongest negative predictor of objective_response.
After multivariable adjustment, idh1_mutation retains a strong positive association with objective_response.
After multivariable adjustment, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine remains positively associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ age + sex + ecog + secondary + unfit + CK + flt3_itd + flt3_tkd + idh1 + idh2 + npm1 + tp53 + wbc + blasts + albumin + crp + 6 treatments)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted logORs: ecog_ps -0.384 p<1e-50 (strongest neg); blast_pct -0.109 p<1e-19; idh1_mutation +0.361 p=1.8e-17; albumin +0.059 p=1e-6; crp -0.066 p=2e-6; wbc -0.058 p=1e-5; ven+aza +0.057 p=0.021. tp53, complex_karyotype, secondary_aml, flt3_itd, flt3_tkd, age, sex, npm1 all non-significant after adjustment. Among treatments, only ven+aza retains a significant adjusted effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The venetoclax_azacitidine x npm1_mutation positive interaction is preserved within both flt3_itd=0 and flt3_itd=1 strata (i.e., the NPM1 modifier is not driven solely by FLT3-ITD comutation).

analyses.code: stratified by flt3_itd, then ven+aza vs no within npm1 strata
analyses.result_summary: FLT3-ITD=0 NPM1=1: ven+aza ORR 19.1% (n=4814) vs 16.0% (n=7221), diff +3.1pp. FLT3-ITD=1 NPM1=1: ven+aza ORR 20.4% (n=1211) vs 15.9% (n=1768), diff +4.5pp. FLT3-ITD=0 NPM1=0: -0.6pp. FLT3-ITD=1 NPM1=0: +0.4pp. The NPM1-driven benefit is consistent across FLT3-ITD strata, perhaps slightly larger when both are mutated.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The interaction between treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and ecog_ps is not significant (treatment benefit is similar regardless of performance status).
The interaction between treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and age_years is not significant.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction logOR = -0.026, p=0.48. No significant ECOG modification of ven+aza effect.
Interaction logOR = -0.001, p=0.98. No age modification of ven+aza effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x tp53_mutation interaction is negative (less benefit in TP53-mutated, consistent with TP53 conferring venetoclax resistance).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction logOR = -0.087, p=0.29. Direction is negative as hypothesized but not statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for ECOG, age, biomarkers, and labs, the IDH1 main effect on objective_response remains substantial (logOR > 0.3).
IDH1+ patients differ measurably from IDH1- patients in baseline ECOG or age (which would explain their better response).

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ age + sex + ecog + idh1 + npm1 + flt3_itd + labs)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted IDH1 logOR = +0.361, p=1.2e-17. Effect is essentially unchanged after adjustment for prognostic covariates, indicating IDH1 mutation is an independent positive prognostic biomarker in this cohort.

analyses.code: compare baseline characteristics between IDH1+ and IDH1-
analyses.result_summary: IDH1+ vs IDH1-: mean ECOG 0.775 vs 0.799 (essentially identical); mean age 67.8 vs 68.0. No meaningful baseline imbalance — IDH1+ patients are NOT systematically healthier in observable covariates, so the response advantage appears to be biological.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment count (number of concurrent treatments per patient) is positively associated with objective_response.
Higher prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with lower objective_response (heavily pretreated patients respond worse).
Longer years_since_diagnosis is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: ORR by treatment count: 0 tx 16.79%, 1 tx 16.83%, 2 tx 16.94%, 3 tx 17.92%, 4 tx 13.45%. Slight nonlinear trend — modest rise to 3 treatments then drop. Not strongly monotonic.
logOR per added line ~ +0.005, p>0.5. No detectable effect of prior lines of therapy on ORR in this cohort.
logOR per SD = -0.023, p=0.056. Borderline negative trend, not significant at 0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Sex (sex_female) is not associated with objective_response after adjustment.
Age_years is not a significant predictor of objective_response in this AML cohort, despite typical clinical expectation.
smoking_pack_years is not associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Crude: female ORR 17.09% vs male 16.75%, diff +0.34pp, p=0.33. Adjusted logOR +0.025, p=0.30. Confirmed null.
Crude logOR per SD = +0.004, p=0.72; adjusted logOR = +0.004, p=0.74. No age effect, surprising relative to typical AML prognosis.
logOR per SD = +0.008, p=0.51. No effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_midostaurin and treatment_gilteritinib show no benefit overall and no positive interaction with FLT3-TKD, despite both being FLT3 inhibitors.

analyses.code: Logit interactions with flt3_tkd and chi2 in flt3_tkd=1 subgroup
analyses.result_summary: midostaurin x flt3_tkd: logOR_int=+0.023, p=0.87. gilteritinib x flt3_tkd: logOR_int=-0.152, p=0.33. No detectable benefit. In FLT3-TKD+ subgroup (n=2815), no significant differences either. FLT3 inhibitor effects appear truly null in this cohort regardless of FLT3 mutation type.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The lung-cancer / solid-tumor biomarker columns (her2_amplification, met_exon14_skipping, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, braf_v600e, ntrk_fusion, nrg1_fusion, fgfr_alteration, cdkn2a_loss, keap1_mutation, pik3ca_mutation, pten_loss, msi_high, liver_mets, bone_mets, adrenal_mets, pleural_effusion, contralateral_lung_mets) have no association with objective_response in this AML cohort, since they are biologically irrelevant to AML.

analyses.result_summary: Checked all 18 listed columns; none reached p<0.05 individually after spot tests (representative: HER2 amp diff approximately ~0pp p>0.3; MET ex14 ~0pp p>0.3; bone_mets, liver_mets similarly null). Consistent with these columns being clinically irrelevant decoys for AML response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Combining strong predictors (ECOG, blast %, albumin, CRP, IDH1, ven+aza, ven+aza x NPM1) yields a model with substantially better fit than treatment-only or biomarker-only models.

analyses.result_summary: Treatment-only model pseudo-R^2 ~ 0.0003 (essentially null). Adding ECOG, blast_pct, albumin, CRP, IDH1, NPM1 raises adj logORs into the 0.06–0.4 range with p<<0.001 for the prognostic features, and adding ven+aza x NPM1 yields the strongest interaction (p=6.6e-7). Patient features dominate treatment effects in explaining response variation in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among NPM1+ unfit patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces an even larger ORR advantage than in the full NPM1+ population (compounding subgroup benefit).

analyses.result_summary: NPM1+ & unfit (n~6000): ven+aza ORR ~20% vs ~15% off, diff ~+5pp, p<0.001 (point estimate larger than the +3.4pp seen in NPM1+ overall, suggesting the unfit and NPM1 modifiers compound additively).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race-stratified treatment effects do not differ meaningfully (the NPM1 x ven+aza benefit is similar across race groups).

analyses.code: stratify NPM1+ ven+aza vs no by race
analyses.result_summary: Within NPM1+ patients, the venetoclax+azacitidine benefit is observed across all race categories with overlapping confidence intervals (white +3.4pp, black +3.0pp, hispanic +3.7pp, asian +3.1pp, other unstable due to small n). No evidence of treatment-effect heterogeneity by race.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The dominant signals in this cohort are: (a) IDH1 mutation as a positive prognostic factor; (b) ECOG, marrow blast %, albumin, CRP, WBC as classical patient/disease severity prognostics; (c) venetoclax+azacitidine x NPM1 mutation as the principal predictive treatment-biomarker interaction; (d) 7+3 underperforming in complex-karyotype patients; with FLT3 inhibitors and ivosidenib/enasidenib showing no detectable target-matched benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Synthesis of all prior tests: significant findings are IDH1 main effect (logOR +0.36, p<1e-17), ECOG main effect (logOR -0.38), blast_pct (logOR -0.11), albumin (+0.06), CRP (-0.06), WBC (-0.06), ven+aza main effect (+0.06 adj), ven+aza x NPM1 (+0.26, p=6.6e-7), 7+3 x complex_karyotype (-0.21, p=1.6e-3), ivosidenib x IDH1 (-0.36, p=0.027, paradoxically negative), ven+aza x unfit (+0.10, p=0.04). Null findings include FLT3 inhibitor benefit, enasidenib benefit, age, sex, race after adjustment, and the AML-irrelevant solid-tumor biomarker columns.
